prompt: Set up a RoadLink Mobility Commerce Platform that enables riders, drivers, and local fleet operators to manage carpool trips, vehicle listings, and optional add-on products in a single offline-capable web application. The Vue.js front end presents role-based workspaces: Riders create a trip request by entering origin/destination as free-form addresses (no map dependency), rider count (1–6), a time window (e.g., 03/25/2026 4:00 PM–6:00 PM), and notes such as luggage size; they then track a clear order timeline from created to matching, accepted, in progress, completed, canceled, or exception, with inline prompts when reassignment occurs. Drivers see a queue of nearby-in-time requests and can accept, start, complete, or flag an exception, while group rides can open an in-group chat that includes system notices (group formed/changed/disbanded) and optional read receipts and Do Not Disturb hours (default 10:00 PM–7:00 AM). Vehicle and product managers upload vehicle photos/videos, choose a cover image, reorder gallery media via drag-and-drop, and maintain product catalogs with multi-variant SKUs (size/package tiers), tiered pricing (e.g., $19.99 for 1–2 units, $17.99 for 3+), purchase limits per user per day, publish/unpublish controls, and inventory strategies (presale with a ship/hand-off date, live stock, or shared inventory across variants). All users share an in-app notification center for comments, replies, @mentions, new followers, moderation outcomes, and system announcements, with unread badges and deduplicated aggregates such as '5 new replies,' plus opt-in subscription-style alerts constrained to a maximum of 3 high-priority alerts per hour. The backend uses Laravel to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the decoupled Vue.js UI, enforcing server-side validation, authorization, and durable workflows in MySQL. Authentication is strictly local username/password with a minimum 10-character password, complexity rules (at least one letter and one number), a 15-minute lockout after 5 failed attempts, and a 12-hour session expiration; sensitive fields (password hashes, any stored contact info) are encrypted or masked at rest. Multimedia asset management stores files on local disk-backed object storage with SHA-256 deduplication, strict MIME/extension validation (JPEG/PNG up to 8 MB; MP4 up to 200 MB), lightweight compression/transcoding presets executed locally, and hotlink protection via short-lived signed download URLs (default 10 minutes) plus permission checks per asset. The ride order state machine is transactionally enforced with idempotent transitions; matching requests auto-cancel after 10 minutes without acceptance, and accepted trips auto-revert to matching after a 5-minute no-show start threshold, with all transitions audited. The hybrid recommendation engine runs fully offline using scheduled batch jobs: multi-channel recall (collaborative filtering plus content similarity on tags/categories), score fusion with diversity constraints (no more than 2 results from the same seller in the top 10), and exploration using epsilon-greedy with e=0.10; models and feature tables are versioned in MySQL for reproducibility. Notifications and group messages are stored locally with read/unread sync, per-type frequency caps (e.g., 20 non-critical notifications per day), and an extensible adapter layer for email/SMS that remains disabled by default to preserve offline operation. Visualization and report export generate trend and distribution charts from MySQL aggregates and provide reusable templates that export to CSV/XLSX files saved to an administrator-selected local directory, including region summaries based on locally bundled reference data rather than any online map service.
